topic: An athlete is asked to switch positions
raw_output:
[story 1]

The coach’s voice, usually a steady anchor, felt like a sudden, sharp wave crashing over me. "Liam, we need you to try playing center for the next match. It's a change of pace." My stomach clenched, a tight, cold knot forming just beneath my ribs. I stared at the polished floor, the reflection of my own face distorted in the sheen of the hardwood. Every muscle in my body felt suddenly rigid, refusing to cooperate. I mumbled something that sounded suspiciously like, "I don't know," my throat suddenly dry. The weight of expectation settled on my shoulders, heavy and suffocating. I kept wanting to bolt, to find any escape from this spotlight, but my feet were rooted to the spot.

[story 2]

"Just try it, Marcus," the trainer urged, clapping his hands together with too much enthusiasm. My palms were slick against the clipboard in my hand. Looking across the court, the opposing team seemed impossibly close, their movements fluid and confident. A tremor started in my hands, a faint buzzing sensation that radiated up my arms. I tried to focus on the rhythm of the drill, but the familiar patterns felt alien, wrong. My breath hitched in my chest, shallow and quick. *What if I mess up? What if I look foolish?* The silence in my head was deafening, filled only with the frantic drumming of my own pulse against my ears.

[story 3]

When the announcement came, the air in the locker room seemed to thin out. I could feel a heat rising in my cheeks, a flush that had nothing to do with exertion. I kept fiddling with the drawstring of my shorts, my fingers tracing the frayed edge repeatedly. The idea of stepping into that unfamiliar role—the one that demanded a different kind of strength—made my knees feel strangely weak. I forced myself to look at the coach, trying to project an air of calm, but my gaze kept darting away, searching for an exit. Every nerve ending seemed hyper-aware, screaming warnings that I couldn't quite decipher. I took a deep, ragged breath, the effort to control my physical response exhausting me.
